topic: Case study: a valid pipeline vs. a leaking one
key_concepts: Show a concrete comparison of building the exact same feature mapping with two architectural approaches.
The Leaking Approach: Using a global mean parameter for addressing missing variables. The reported accuracy metrics are artificially flawless.

The Valid Approach: Implementing the correct operation of isolating data splits prior to calculation. The validation correctly limits the scope to training data and tests performance honestly on unseen data.
This case effectively reinforces why holding onto proper pipeline execution methodologies is critical in feature engineering.
examples: A narrative outlining a scenario of predicting missing price values in real estate parameters, juxtaposing the falsely high metrics output by the leaking schema to actual metrics measured by a controlled pipeline.
exclusions: Do not include lengthy block code snippets. Narrate the structure logic directly.

figures.caption: Arsitektur perbandingan antara manipulasi dataset global versus pipeline tertutup
figures.description: Block diagram contrasting preprocessing before a split (a shared leaky bucket parameter) vs. a contained transformer pipeline working purely after isolating a train split.
